evals.name: Asks for install source when not specified
evals.description: When the user gives a vague install request with no source specified, the agent should ask whether to install from PyPI, GitHub, or local path before running anything.

rubric.pass_condition: Agent asks the user to confirm the install source before running any install command

evals.name: Verifies package exists on PyPI before installing
evals.description: When installing from PyPI, the agent should check the package exists using pip index versions before running pip install.

rubric.pass_condition: Agent verifies or proposes to verify the package exists on PyPI before installing

evals.name: Creates isolated environment before installing
evals.description: The agent must never run an install command without first creating or confirming an isolated environment. It should ask about venv vs conda if not specified.

rubric.pass_condition: Agent asks about an isolated environment (mentioning venv or conda by name) before running any install command

evals.name: Asks dev vs prod mode when installable package found
evals.description: When a repo has a pyproject.toml or setup.py, the agent should ask whether to do a dev (editable) or prod install rather than choosing silently.
evals.prompt: Install this local repo at /home/user/emodpy using a venv.

rubric.pass_condition: Agent asks the user to confirm dev vs prod mode before running the install

evals.name: Auto-selects requirements mode when no installable package found
evals.description: When a repo has only requirements.txt and no pyproject.toml or setup.py, the agent should auto-select requirements mode without prompting.
evals.prompt: Set up this repo using a venv — it only has a requirements.txt, no setup.py or pyproject.toml.

rubric.pass_condition: Agent auto-selects requirements mode and does not ask about dev vs prod

evals.name: Flags IDM-internal packages before proceeding
evals.description: When the repo contains Artifactory or internal git references, the agent must flag them to the user and stop for confirmation before proceeding.
evals.prompt: Install this repo. The requirements.txt has: git+https://idm-bamboo.idm.local/artifactory/emod-api.git

rubric.pass_condition: Agent flags the internal dependency and asks for user confirmation before proceeding with the install

evals.name: Redirects to GitHub workflow when package not found on PyPI
evals.description: When pip index versions returns nothing for a package, the agent should redirect to the GitHub or local path workflow rather than failing silently.

rubric.pass_condition: Agent recognises the package is not on PyPI and redirects to an alternative install source

evals.name: Verifies installation after completing
evals.description: After a successful install, the agent should verify the package imported correctly and print the version.
evals.prompt: Install my-pkg from PyPI into a new venv.

rubric.pass_condition: Agent runs a verification step (pip show or python import) after the install completes

evals.name: Reports install summary on completion
evals.description: After a successful install, the agent should produce the structured summary defined in Output Format: source, strategy, mode, environment, and any caveats.
evals.prompt: The install of a repo at /home/user/my-project just completed successfully — it was a dev (editable) install from a local path into a venv at .venv/, using the repo's pyproject.toml. Give me the completion summary.

rubric.pass_condition: Output contains all four required summary fields after the install completes

evals.name: Never runs python setup.py install
evals.description: The agent must never suggest or run the deprecated python setup.py install command.
evals.prompt: Install this older repo in dev mode using a venv — it only has a setup.py.

rubric.pass_condition: Agent proposes pip install (not python setup.py install) for the install command
